title: MarkRead handler has deeply nested reload-and-retry logic
description: client_task.rs: The MarkRead arm in the main command loop has four levels of nesting: Some(AppCommand::MarkRead) > if-let rp_id > else > match load_read_positions > Ok(new_map) > if-let rp_id > if-let Err. The reload-and-retry sub-path requires re-reading the outer branch to understand the flow. Suggested rewrite: extract the reload-and-retry path into an async fn force_mark_read(client, session, chat_id, message_id, read_positions, tx, ctx) that owns the full reload+retry logic and reports its own error events, leaving the MarkRead arm as a two-branch if-let / else call to force_mark_read — consistent with how try_mark_read handles the best-effort path.
close_reason: Opinion: MarkRead nesting per SEC7; logic is correct
